meta.wire_notation: Format: [mm²][colour1]/[colour2]. Example: 1.0 ro/sw = 1.0mm² red with black stripe. Dimension before colour on diagram.

unit_notes.track: Current track number (vertical column in diagram)
unit_notes.DXA: Not used here - reference only for docx skill

diagram_reading_guide.title: Sample current flow diagram with explanatory notes
notes: All switches and contacts are illustrated in the mechanical OFF position.
The grey area represents the relay plate with fuse holder.
Wire cross section in mm² is shown as a number on the wire (e.g. 0.5, 1.0, 1.5, 2.5, 4.0, 6.0, 16.0, 25.0, 35.0).
Wiring colours are shown in the actual colours found on the vehicle.
Numbers/number combinations indicate individual contacts in a multi-point connector, e.g. T10/4 = ten-point connector, contact 4.
Numbers in yellow squares indicate that a wire is discontinued in the diagram and refer to the current track where it is continued.
Terminals with numbers are the numbers on the actual parts (e.g. 15, 30, 31, 50, 56, 58).
Symbols (e.g. W = interior light) – using the legend you can find which part is referred to.
Numbers in circles indicate the locations of earthing points (see legend).
Current track numbers at the bottom help find parts in the diagram (see legend).
Pictorial illustrations of parts whose symbols are given in the current track above.
Internal connections (thin lines) are NOT found in the form of wires. They are however current-carrying connections, making it possible to check the flow of current within a component or unit.

symbols.title: Symbols used in current flow diagrams

symbol_list.symbol_description: Sender unit for coolant temperature gauge
Relay (electronically controlled) – box with K and dashed coil
Relay (with diode) – relay box with diode symbol
Cigarette lighter – plug symbol with heating element
Heated rear window – rectangle with zigzag element
Interior light (circle with X and switch arms)
Spark gap (two arrows pointing at each other)
Wiring connection, detachable (open circle on wire)
Wiring connection, fixed (filled circle on wire)
Crossed wires (not connected) – wires cross without dot

ignition_starter_switch.title: Ignition/starter switch viewed from below
ignition_starter_switch.description: Terminal layout of ignition/starter switch (refer to Wiring Diagram)

terminals.function: Accessory circuit (switched, also in accessory position)
To alternator warning light / charge indicator

ignition_starter_switch.notes: The switch controls which power rails are energised. Terminal 30 is always live from battery. Terminal 15 provides switched ignition power to fuses/relays. Terminal X provides accessory power.

earth_points.title: Earthing points – location and function
earth_points.description: Satisfactory operation of the starter motor, ignition system and electrical accessories depends upon the provision of clean, secure earth bonds.

earth_point_table_fig_13_77_4cyl.location: Earthing strap from engine to body
Earthing point underneath instrument panel near fusebox
Earthing point, on roof crossmember, passenger compartment
Earthing point, at rear of longitudinal member
Earthing point, on longitudinal member, rear left
Earthing point, on longitudinal member, rear right

earth_point_table_fig_13_78_6cyl.location: Earth point next to relay plate
Earth point on dash panel insert
Earth point behind dash panel, right
Earth point on left hand longitudinal member, rear
Earth point on rear crossmember, left
Earth point on rear crossmember, right
Earth point on front roof rail
Earth point near interior light, rear

symptoms.symptom: Starter motor fails to turn engine
reasons: Battery terminal leads loose or earth lead not securely attached to body
Loose or broken connections in starter motor circuit
Starter motor switch or solenoid faulty
Starter brushes badly worn, sticking, or brush wires loose

symptoms.symptom: Starter motor turns engine very slowly
reasons: Starter brushes badly worn, sticking, or brush wires loose
Loose wires in starter motor circuit

symptoms.symptom: Starter motor operates without turning engine
reasons: Pinion or flywheel gear teeth broken or worn

symptoms.symptom: Starter motor noisy or has an excessively rough engagement
reasons: Pinion or flywheel gear teeth broken or worn

symptoms.symptom: Battery will not hold charge for more than a few days
reasons: Electrolyte level too low or electrolyte too weak due to leakage
Plate separators no longer fully effective
Battery terminal connections loose or corroded
Short in lighting circuit causing continual battery drain

symptoms.symptom: Ignition light fails to go out; battery runs flat in a few days
reasons: Alternator belt loose and slipping or broken
Cable between tank sender unit and gauge earthed or loose

symptoms.symptom: Fuel gauge registers full all the time
reasons: Cable between tank unit and gauge broken or disconnected
Horn push either earthed or stuck down
Horn cable to horn push earthed
Cable or cable connection loose, broken or disconnected

symptoms.symptom: Horn emits intermittent or unsatisfactory noise

reasons: If engine not running, battery discharged
Light bulb filament burnt out or bulbs broken
Wire connections loose, disconnected or broken
Light switch shorting or otherwise faulty

symptoms.symptom: Lights come on but fade out
reasons: If engine not running, battery discharged
Incorrect bulb with too low wattage fitted
Existing bulbs old and badly discoloured
Electrical wiring too thin not allowing full current to pass

symptoms.symptom: Lights work erratically – flashing on and off, especially over bumps
reasons: Battery terminals or earth connection loose
Wire connections loose, disconnected or broken

symptoms.symptom: Wiper motor works very slowly and takes excessive current
reasons: Drive to wheelboxes bent or unlubricated

symptoms.symptom: Wiper motor works slowly and takes little current
Wiper motor works but wiper blades remain static
reasons: Wheelbox gear and spindle damaged or worn
Wiper motor gearbox parts badly worn

washer_system.title: Electric washer system

headlamp_jet_adjustment.setting: 24.0 mm (0.94 in) from centre of jet to centre of lamp lens

dimensions.c: Manual 50.0 mm (1.96 in), electric 200.0 mm (7.9 in)
dimensions.d: Manual 50.0 mm (1.96 in), electric 200.0 mm (7.9 in)

maintenance_notes: All later models have electrically-operated windscreen washer.
Some versions also have headlamp washers.
Early models have a foot-operated pump.
Maintain fluid level and use washer cleaning liquid or methylated spirit in freezing temperatures.
Never add cooling system antifreeze to washer fluid.
Check security of washer tubes and electrical leads.

notes: Certain models are fitted with a driving cab heated rear window.
A relay for this electrical accessory is located on the fuse block.
Avoid scratching the heater element and do not stick labels over it.
Clean the glass with water only, and rub in the direction of the element heater strips.
It may be possible to repair a break in the heater element by using a proprietary conductive paint. Failing this, renewal of the window will be necessary.
